spec.description: Aggregate memory usage across the entire cluster. The 'used' line shows total consumed memory, while the dashed 'total' line shows the aggregate physical memory available. The gap between them indicates free cluster memory.
spec.title: Cluster memory usage

spec.description: Per-host CPU usage percentage over time. Shows how much of each host's available CPU capacity is being consumed by VMs and hypervisor overhead.
spec.title: Host CPU usage %

spec.description: Per-host CPU ready time in milliseconds. CPU ready time measures how long a virtual machine waits to be scheduled on a physical CPU. High values indicate CPU overcommitment and resource contention.
spec.title: Host CPU ready (ms) — contention indicator

spec.description: Per-host physical CPU (PCPU) weighted utilization percentage. Unlike simple usage, this metric accounts for the ratio of demand to available physical cores, giving a more accurate view of host CPU saturation.
spec.title: Host CPU utilization % (PCPU-weighted)

spec.description: Per-host CPU core utilization percentage averaged across all cores. Shows how heavily the individual CPU cores on each host are being utilized.
spec.title: Host CPU core utilization %

spec.description: Breakdown of CPU time spent in wait (I/O wait) and idle states in milliseconds. High wait values may indicate storage or network I/O bottlenecks; idle indicates unused capacity.
spec.title: Host CPU wait/idle breakdown

spec.description: Per-host memory usage in bytes, stacked to show the contribution of each host to the total. Useful for identifying hosts with disproportionate memory consumption.
spec.title: Host memory usage

spec.description: Per-host memory usage as a percentage of total installed memory. Helps identify hosts approaching capacity limits (threshold: 80%+).
spec.title: Host memory usage %

spec.description: Per-host active memory in bytes. Active memory represents the amount of memory that has been recently accessed by VMs and services. A key metric for understanding true memory demand.
spec.title: Host active memory

spec.description: Memory reclaim metrics showing balloon driver usage, swap-out, and compressed memory in bytes. Ballooning indicates memory overcommitment; swapping and compression suggest severe memory pressure.
spec.title: Host ballooned / swapped / compressed

spec.description: Maximum memory pressure state across all hosts (0 = low, 1 = medium, 2 = high, 3 = severe). Derived from ESXi memory state counters. Values above 0 indicate memory contention.
spec.title: Memory pressure state (max)

spec.description: Top 10 disk devices by read latency in milliseconds. High read latency (>10-15ms for spinning disks, >2-5ms for SSDs) may indicate storage performance issues.
spec.title: Disk read latency (ms) — top 10 devices

spec.description: Top 10 disk devices by write latency in milliseconds. High write latency can indicate storage array back-pressure, contention, or insufficient write cache.
spec.title: Disk write latency (ms) — top 10 devices

spec.description: Total disk IOPS (read + write operations per second) aggregated per host. Shows storage I/O activity levels across the cluster.
spec.title: Disk IOPS — read+write

spec.description: Disk read and write throughput in bytes per second, aggregated per host. Shows how much data is being read from and written to storage devices.
spec.title: Disk throughput

spec.description: Network receive throughput in bytes per second, aggregated per host. Measures incoming network traffic from all physical NICs.
spec.title: Network throughput per host (RX)

spec.description: Network transmit throughput in bytes per second, aggregated per host. Measures outgoing network traffic from all physical NICs.
spec.title: Network throughput per host (TX)

spec.description: NIC link state timeline showing whether each physical NIC link is up (1 = OK) or down (0 = Err). Active links should consistently show 'OK'; errors indicate cabling or switch port issues.
spec.title: NIC link state — should stay at 1

spec.description: NIC link speed timeline showing the negotiated speed of each physical NIC. Values show 0 (Offline), 100 Mbps (red), or 1 Gbps (green). Drops in speed indicate physical layer problems.
spec.title: NIC link speed

spec.description: Cumulative dropped network packets (receive + transmit) per NIC over time. A persistent increase indicates network congestion, buffer exhaustion, or faulty hardware.
spec.title: Dropped packets (RX+TX)

spec.description: Host CPU model information table showing the processor make, model, and core count for each host in the inventory.
spec.title: Host inventory

spec.description: Count of hosts that require a reboot (e.g., after patching or firmware updates). A non-zero value indicates hosts with pending reboots.
spec.title: Hosts with reboot required

spec.description: Count of hosts currently in lockdown mode. Lockdown mode restricts direct root access to ESXi hosts, enforcing centralized management through vCenter.
spec.title: Hosts in lockdown

spec.description: Count of hosts with hyperthreading enabled. Hyperthreading allows each physical core to handle multiple threads. Disabling it halves logical CPU count.
spec.title: Hyperthreading active (hosts)

spec.description: Count of hosts with UEFI secure boot enabled. Secure boot ensures only signed code can boot the host, protecting against bootkit and firmware attacks.
spec.title: Secure boot enabled (hosts)

spec.description: Table of host uptime displayed in seconds (raw value from ESXi). Use this to assess host stability and identify recently rebooted hosts. A host with less than 86400 seconds (1 day) of uptime has been restarted recently.
spec.title: Host uptime (days)

spec.description: CPU temperature heatmap showing thermal sensor readings in degrees Celsius across all hosts. Helps identify overheating CPUs that may throttle or fail.
spec.title: CPU temperature (°C)

spec.description: DIMM memory temperature heatmap in degrees Celsius. High DIMM temperatures can cause memory errors, system instability, and hardware failure.
spec.title: DIMM temperature (°C)

spec.description: Fan speed sensor readings in RPM across all hosts. Decreasing fan speeds or unusual values may indicate fan failure or cooling system issues.
spec.title: Fan speed (RPM)

spec.description: Voltage sensor readings in volts across all hosts. Out-of-range voltages can indicate power supply issues or motherboard problems.
spec.title: Voltage (V)

spec.description: Table of hardware sensors reporting non-healthy (non-green) status. Includes sensors for temperature, voltage, fan, and power that have exceeded normal thresholds.
spec.title: Non-green sensors

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by CPU demand in MHz. Helps identify VMs with the highest processing requirements.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by CPU usage (MHz)

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by CPU ready time (contention indicator). High ready times mean the VM is waiting for CPU resources due to overcommitment.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by CPU ready (contention)

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by CPU wait time in milliseconds. High wait times suggest the VM is frequently stalled waiting for I/O operations to complete.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by CPU wait

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by co-stop time (SMP contention). Co-stop occurs when vCPUs of a multi-vCPU VM wait for each other to be scheduled. High values indicate SMP scheduling inefficiency.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by CPU co-stop (SMP contention)

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by consumed memory in bytes. Consumed memory includes all memory allocated to the VM, including overhead.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by memory consumed

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by ballooned memory (values > 0 only). Ballooned memory is reclaimed by the ESXi balloon driver under memory pressure. Any non-zero value indicates host memory overcommitment.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by ballooned memory

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by swapped memory (values > 0 only). Swapping occurs when the host must page VM memory to disk — a severe memory pressure indicator that should be addressed promptly.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by swapped memory

spec.description: Time series of consumed memory for all VMs (not just top 10). Useful for spotting memory trends across the entire VM fleet.
spec.title: Consumed Memory By VMs

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by disk read IOPS. Identifies VMs with the highest volume of storage read operations.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by disk read IOPS

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by disk write IOPS. Identifies VMs with the highest volume of storage write operations.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by disk write IOPS

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by maximum disk latency in milliseconds. Helps pinpoint VMs experiencing storage performance degradation.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by disk latency

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by combined disk throughput (read + write) in bytes per second. Shows which VMs are moving the most data to and from storage.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by disk throughput (read+write)

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by network receive throughput. Identifies VMs receiving the most inbound network traffic.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by network RX

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by network transmit throughput. Identifies VMs sending the most outbound network traffic.
spec.title: Top 10 VMs by network TX

spec.description: Top 10 VMs with dropped network packets (> 0). Dropped packets can indicate VM network driver issues, virtual switch congestion, or oversubscribed bandwidth.
spec.title: Dropped packets per VM

spec.description: Table of VMs with outdated or missing VMware Tools (status < 1). VMware Tools are essential for guest shutdown, snapshot quiescing, and performance monitoring.
spec.title: VMs with old/missing VMware Tools

spec.description: Table of VMs with a red (non-responsive) guest heartbeat status (status == 3). A red heartbeat indicates the guest OS has stopped responding, which may signal a guest OS crash or hang.
spec.title: VMs with red heartbeat

spec.description: Table of VMs with guest filesystems exceeding 85% capacity usage. Full or near-full filesystems can cause application errors, data loss, and VM instability.
spec.title: Guest filesystems > 85% used

spec.description: Table of VMs with snapshots older than 1 day. Long-lived snapshots consume disk space, degrade performance, and risk datastore filling up.
spec.title: Snapshots older than 1 day (present count)

spec.description: Total number of datastores discovered across the vSphere inventory. Reflects how many storage volumes are available to the cluster.
spec.title: Datastores

spec.description: Table mapping VMs to their IP addresses. Useful for quickly finding the network address of any virtual machine.
spec.title: VM → IP addresses

spec.description: Datastore free space percentage with an alert threshold at 15%. Helps identify datastores approaching capacity. Values below 15% require attention to prevent VM stuns from datastore-full conditions.
spec.title: Datastore free % — alert threshold at 15%

spec.description: Datastore capacity (total) vs used space in bytes over time. Shows how quickly each datastore is being consumed.
spec.title: Datastore capacity vs used

spec.description: Table summarizing each datastore's total capacity and available free space. Quick reference for storage planning.
spec.title: Datastore summary

spec.description: Datastore read latency in milliseconds with a 5-minute averaging window. Persistent high latency indicates storage subsystem performance issues.
spec.title: Datastore read latency (5-min rollup)

spec.description: Datastore write latency in milliseconds with a 5-minute averaging window. Elevated write latency can impact VM write performance and application responsiveness.
spec.title: Datastore write latency (5-min rollup)

spec.description: Datastore IOPS showing the total input/output operations per second per datastore. Useful for understanding storage workload distribution.
spec.title: Datastore IOPS

spec.description: Datastore read and write throughput in bytes per second. Shows data transfer rates for each datastore.
spec.title: Datastore throughput

spec.description: Total number of vSphere clusters discovered in the inventory. Clusters aggregate hosts and enable HA, DRS, and other advanced vSphere features.
spec.title: Clusters

spec.description: Count of clusters that have vSphere High Availability (HA) enabled. HA provides automatic VM restart on host failure.
spec.title: Clusters with HA on

spec.description: Count of active red (critical) alarms currently firing in vSphere. Red alarms indicate serious issues requiring immediate attention.
spec.title: Red Alarms

spec.description: Count of clusters that have Distributed Resource Scheduler (DRS) enabled. DRS balances compute load by recommending or automatically migrating VMs.
spec.title: Clusters with DRS on

spec.description: Total number of virtual machines across all clusters. Provides a quick view of the overall VM footprint.
spec.title: Total cluster VMs

spec.description: Per-cluster CPU utilization percentage over time. Shows how busy each cluster's hosts are relative to total available CPU capacity.
spec.title: Cluster CPU utilization

spec.description: Per-cluster memory utilization percentage over time. Shows how much of each cluster's total memory is being consumed.
spec.title: Cluster memory utilization

spec.description: Table of resource pool CPU usage (MHz) and memory usage (bytes) vs their configured limits. Helps identify resource pools approaching or exceeding their allocation.
spec.title: Resource pool usage

spec.description: Count of active red (critical) alarms currently firing in vSphere. Red alarms indicate serious issues requiring immediate attention.
spec.title: Red alarms

spec.description: Count of active yellow (warning) alarms currently firing in vSphere. Yellow alarms indicate potential issues that should be investigated.
spec.title: Yellow alarms

spec.description: Count of currently firing gray (unknown) alarms. Gray alarms typically indicate a loss of connectivity or monitoring state uncertainty.
spec.title: Gray alarms

spec.description: Count of vCenter events in the last 5-minute window. Spikes in event activity may correlate with infrastructure changes or incidents.
spec.title: vCenter events (5m window)

spec.description: Table of currently firing alarms with detailed information including alarm name, object, and description. Use this to investigate and remediate active issues.
spec.title: Currently firing alarms

spec.description: Count of active yellow (warning) alarms currently firing in vSphere. Yellow alarms indicate potential issues that should be investigated.
spec.title: Yellow Alarms

spec.description: Total VM count vs powered-on VM count over time. The gap between the two lines shows how many VMs are powered off or suspended.
spec.title: VM Count

spec.description: Host count breakdown over time: powered-off/unreachable hosts, powered-on hosts, and hosts in maintenance mode. Helps track host availability status.
spec.title: Hosts

spec.description: Treemap visualization of VMs grouped by guest operating system. Each block represents a VM, color-coded and sized by OS type. Useful for understanding the OS distribution across the VM fleet.
spec.title: VMs By OS

spec.description: Count of hosts currently powered on and running (power state == 1). Quick health check for host availability.
spec.title: Running Hosts

spec.description: Gauge showing the overall cluster memory usage percentage. Calculated as total used memory divided by total available memory across all hosts.
spec.title: Memory Usage

spec.description: Gauge showing the maximum CPU usage percentage across all hosts. Represents the most heavily utilized host in the cluster.
spec.title: CPU Usage

spec.description: Gauge showing the total free storage percentage. Calculated as total free datastore space divided by total datastore capacity.
spec.title: Free Storage

spec.description: Top 10 VMs ranked by active memory in bytes. Active memory represents memory that has been recently accessed — a more accurate measure of true memory demand than consumed memory.
spec.title: Active Memory By VMs (Top 10)

spec.description: Cluster-wide CPU usage showing the average and maximum CPU utilization percentage across all selected hosts. Helps identify overall cluster load balance and hot spots.
spec.title: Cluster CPU utilization %

spec.description: Time series of active memory for all VMs. Active memory reflects pages that have been recently touched by the guest OS, providing insight into actual memory working sets.
spec.title: Active Memory By VMs
